description: SkillWeave SAD skill index â€” loaded at session start in place of all full SKILL.md files. The agent uses this to perform Iterative Skill-Aware Decomposition (SAD): decompose a task into atomic sub-tasks, match each to a skill here, re-decompose if unmatched, then load only the matched SKILL.md files.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Explores user intent and requirements through collaborative dialogue, proposes approaches, and produces an approved design spec before any code is written. Use before any creative work â€” creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Creates a detailed implementation plan from an approved spec, breaking work into bite-sized TDD tasks with file-level detail. Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Executes an existing implementation plan task-by-task, using TDD, committing after each passing task. Use when you have an approved plan and need to implement it sequentially.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Implements features or bug fixes using strict red-green-refactor TDD: write a failing test first, watch it fail, write minimal code to pass, refactor. Never write production code without a failing test first.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Investigates bugs, test failures, and unexpected behavior by finding the root cause before attempting any fix. Follows a 4-phase process: root cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and targeted fix.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Executes an implementation plan by dispatching fresh subagents per task, each followed by a two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality). Use when the plan has mostly independent tasks that can be parallelized.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Verifies a deliverable meets all requirements before marking work complete. Covers: tests passing, configuration changes producing correct outcomes, no regressions, and behavioral verification (not just success codes).

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Wraps up a feature branch: final review, changelog, PR creation, merge. Use after verification passes and the feature is ready to ship.

skills.name: browser-testing
skills.description: Tests web UIs using browser automation: taking screenshots, verifying visual output, interacting with UI elements, and confirming the UI matches the spec. Use when verifying frontend features or UI changes.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Runs multiple independent research or work tasks in parallel using concurrent subagents. Use when tasks have no dependencies on each other and can be done simultaneously to save time.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
skills.description: Guides an agent receiving code review feedback: how to process review comments, respond constructively, and make targeted fixes without scope creep.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Prepares and conducts a structured code review: the reviewer reads the diff and files, evaluates spec compliance and code quality, and produces an actionable review report.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Creates and manages git worktrees for isolated feature development â€” each feature branch gets its own working directory so multiple branches can be active simultaneously without context switching.

skills.name: using-superpowers
skills.description: Entry-point skill that establishes how to find and use all other skills. Invokes the SkillWeave SAD orchestrator to decompose any complex request into atomic sub-tasks, retrieve matching skills, and compose a dependency-ordered execution plan.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Guides the creation of new Superpowers skills: how to structure SKILL.md, write effective trigger descriptions, define dependencies, and ensure the skill is atomic and reusable.
triggers: package a workflow as a skill
